LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT YINGCUI LONG'S MOTION TO ENFORCE PARTIES' SIGNED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT UNDER CCP 664.6 AND FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF UNDER CCP 1060 AND AWARD OF PREVAILING PARTY ATTORNEY FEES. SETTLING DEFENDANT YINGCUI TRACY LONG HAS MOVED TO ENFORCE HER SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WITH PLAINTIFF MINGLI WANG. FOR THE REASONS STATED HEREIN, THE COURT FINDS THAT LONG HAS PERFORMED HER OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BY MAKING THE PAYMENT TO WANG IN THE AMOUNT AND MANNER PRESCRIBED BY THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. LONG SHALL PROVIDE A PROPOSED JUDGMENT AS DESCRIBED HEREIN. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A SEPARATE PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND A SEPARATE PROPOSED JUDGMENT. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA) ****RULING VACATED PER ORDER RESETTING HEARING ON MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT. Notice Of Motion To Enforce Parties' Signed Settlement Agreement Under Ccp 664.6 And For Declaratory Relief Under Ccp 1060 And Award Of Prevailing Party Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Nov-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mingli Wang, 510-588-9198 Plaintiff in Pro Per
Ory Cross, Esq., Valor Legal, P.C. (415) 545-8394 Attorney for Defendant LONG
Aidong Ni, Court Certified Mandarin Interperter for Plantiff Wang

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT YINGCUI LONG'S Motion To Enforce Parties' Signed Settlement Agreement Under CCP 664.6 And For Declaratory Relief Under CCP 1060 And Award Of Prevailing Party Attorney Fees.

Settling defendant Yingcui Tracy Long has moved to enforce her settlement agreement with plaintiff Mingli Wang. For the reasons stated herein, the court finds that Long has performed her obligations under the settlement agreement by making the payment to Wang in the amount and manner prescribed by the settlement agreement. Long shall provide a proposed judgment as described herein.

The court incorporates below material from its prior order of October 3 so that there is a single order setting out its decision in full.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a separate proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling AND a separate proposed judgment.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MINI MINUTES:
Attendance:

Attorney arthur Liu, with Plaintiff Mingli Wang being assisted by Mandarin Interpreter, Aidong Ni.

Attorney Michael Noble, with Defendant Stephen West McVeigh.

The matter is before the Honorable Samuel K. Feng, Judge for a mandatory settlement conference. Judge Feng conducts the settlement confenrence on behalf of the Honorable Andrew Y.S. Cheng, Judge who is presiding over the Jury Trial.

Parties come to an agreement. The Court recites the parties' stipulated agreement on the record as stated. The Plaintiff agrees to the terms on the record. The parties agree to the commencement date of payments. Matter is adjourned.
